_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-19 On-Scene Physician Interaction (Revised 3/24/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-19: On-Scene Physician Interaction — MDHHS Approved 3/24/2023
purpose: Govern EMS provider interaction with physicians encountered on scene. An on-scene physician may offer clinical assistance; however, EMS providers are operating under the authority of their medical director and MCA protocols. An on-scene physician does not automatically assume medical control authority. Formal verification and process are required for an on-scene physician to assume medical control.

sections.title: On-Scene Physician — Initial Response
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-19: On-Scene Physician — Initial Response
points: A physician identifying themselves on scene should be politely acknowledged and their assistance offered an appropriate role
Request physician identification: name, medical license, specialty
An on-scene physician may assist with patient care as a team member under EMS direction
An on-scene physician does NOT automatically assume command of the EMS response or medical control authority

sections.title: On-Scene Physician Assuming Medical Control
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-19: On-Scene Physician — Assuming MC
points: An on-scene physician may assume medical control ONLY if ALL of the following are met:
1. The physician identifies themselves by name and medical license
2. The physician is willing to assume full medical and legal responsibility for the patient on scene and during transport
3. The physician accompanies the patient throughout transport if assuming medical control
4. Online medical control is contacted and authorizes the on-scene physician to assume medical control
If any of these conditions are NOT met: continue under standard protocols; contact online MC for guidance
Document: physician name, license number, contact with online MC, and authorization in PCR

sections.title: Conflicting Orders
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-19: On-Scene Physician — Conflicting Orders
points: If an on-scene physician's requested action conflicts with EMS protocol: contact online MC for guidance
EMS providers are NOT obligated to perform skills outside their scope simply because an on-scene physician requests it
If an on-scene physician requests a potentially harmful action: decline; contact online MC immediately
Document all physician-requested actions that conflict with protocol, the MC response, and the ultimate treatment decision

deterioration_flags: On-scene physician requests action outside EMS scope — decline; contact online MC; document
On-scene physician assumes MC without online MC authorization — contact online MC immediately; continue standard protocols until authorization received
scope_notes: On-scene physician does NOT automatically assume medical control
Physician may assist as team member without assuming MC
Assuming MC requires: identification, willingness to accept legal responsibility, accompaniment during transport, AND online MC authorization
Never perform skills outside scope at physician request
Document physician name, license, MC contact, authorization
